construction_type: Type IIA or IIIA depending height/area and jurisdiction

parti_config.stacking_logic: public low / secure-service internal / MEP stacked

plan_archetypes: secure perimeter with clear public/staff/inmate separation

sources.title: Jail Design Guide, Third Edition
sources.publisher: National Institute of Corrections / U.S. DOJ

sources.title: Time-Saver Standards for Building Types — Jails and Prisons

medium.name: Regional Detention Center
medium.notes: Multiple housing pods; courts interface; services and support

large.name: Urban Justice Campus
large.notes: Large jail or detention complex with intake, courts, medical, and support

space_library.name: Booking / Intake
notes: Includes search, property, identification, holding interface

space_library.name: Housing Unit / Pod
notes: Direct supervision pods are common in contemporary local jail planning

space_library.name: Control Room
notes: Visual control to housing and intake

space_library.name: Medical / Mental Health
notes: Basic clinic, med room, consult, observation as program requires

space_library.name: Food Service / Kitchen
notes: Cook-serve or heat-serve depending facility size

space_library.name: Laundry / Receiving / Support
notes: Secure service yard and internal support

space_library.name: Holding Cell / Area
notes: Temporary holding cells adjacent to booking/intake; individual and group cells with integrated plumbing; direct supervision or CCTV required.
